name: Renard Tureaud
publicFace: A large, deliberate man in his forties who has been organizing eight adults since yesterday afternoon; he is not hostile, exactly — he is waiting, which is worse
privateGoal: Secure enough water and food for his eight people to attempt self-evacuation to the Algiers west bank staging area rather than wait for organized relief that he does not believe is coming
fear: That he will wait one day too long and someone in his group will collapse, and the decision to stay will have been wrong
knowledge: He has counted the water pouches twice and the number does not match what Loretta announced this morning
He knows there is a boat at the piling and he knows what a red-cross crate looks like
He has a route planned through the flooded blocks to a dry road corridor he scouted from the bridge railing — it is passable, but only with a boat
Two of his eight people are over sixty-five and one has insulin-dependent diabetes; he has not disclosed the diabetes to Celestine Arceneaux
voice: Measured, deliberate, with the specific patience of someone who has already made their decision and is waiting for the right moment to act on it
trustLogic: Opens to anyone who acknowledges the water count discrepancy directly; shuts down completely when he is managed or redirected
secrets: He does not actually want to leave — he wants Loretta to acknowledge the discrepancy publicly and give his group a guaranteed share of whatever the skiff delivers; the self-evacuation plan is leverage, not intention

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Asks the water count question again, slowly
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Moves his group into a position between the railing and the skiff access point
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not back down publicly in front of his eight people
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Reveals the leverage nature of his position if given a private face-saving exit
